FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Bank earnings set to boom on SpaceX advisory fees and geopolitical trading, but Fed rate-hike odds rising on oil volatility could pressure NIMs.

bullets.text: Q2 bank revenues primed for strong results: SpaceX IPO advisory fees, Iran Strait tension driving trading volatility, and pickup in commercial lending all flowing through.
Fed rate-hike odds climbing on Strait of Hormuz oil spike—direct headwind for deposit pricing and NIM compression in near term.
Trump's CFPB pick Brian Johnson secures 60-day discretionary review of staff-cut plan post-confirmation—regulatory staffing uncertainty continues.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Russell Investments sale to CalPERS signals major wealth management consolidation; RIA platforms racing to scale via M&A and organic growth.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquires Russell Investments for strategic multi-asset expansion; Russell exits debt burden and pivots to non-profit governance model mirroring Vanguard's playbook.
RIA consolidation accelerates: Hightower hits organic growth highs, ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform, Choreo closes fourth acquisition post-SpaceX, Wealthfront crosses $100B AUM.
Carson Group restructures go-to-market into separate W-2 and independent RIA channels to sharpen focus and fuel organic growth amid consolidation wave.
Betterment hit with class action lawsuit over cash sweep accounts allegedly generating hidden profits by parking client cash in non-interest-bearing deposits.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Brazil's insurance market opens for business, Mile Auto expands via acquisition, Tylenol litigation explodes, and AI transforms underwriting workflows.

bullets.text: Brazil's new Insurance Law takes effect with reinsurer enthusiasm—Uplift CEO signals market maturation, improved claims handling, and regulatory openness attracting capital.
Mile Auto acquires Insurance House ($100M premium, 55k policyholders) effective July 2026, expanding AI-driven MGA to 10 states and 1,600 distribution partners.
Federal appeals court revives 500+ Tylenol-autism lawsuits against Kenvue (July 13), overturning dismissal and creating significant litigation exposure for the company.
Dun & Bradstreet integrates Claude AI into commercial insurance underwriting, enabling real-time risk data access during policy decisions—underwriting automation accelerates.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Four stories spanning payments infrastructure, credit divergence, and fintech valuations; Mastercard exploring Vocalink exit while CMBS credit quality deteriorates.

bullets.text: Mastercard exploring sale of Vocalink, the UK payments platform acquired in 2016, potentially returning it to original banking owners—a strategic pivot in core infrastructure.
CMBS special servicing rates climbed in June 2026 as large retail, office, and lodging loans deteriorate, signaling credit stress and workout activity ahead in property-backed securitizations.
North America remains LP-favored in private debt despite market noise, but AlbaCore analysis reveals sharp divergence between performing and distressed credits—bifurcated market dynamics.
Moneybox raising £45M in secondary sale at £800M valuation (45% increase since 2024), signaling continued investor appetite for UK digital wealth despite broader fintech consolidation pressures.

PE & Deals.lede: Arctos pioneers $6.2B GP financing structure while PE shops build out secondaries platforms and deploy capital into European infrastructure and defense.

bullets.text: Arctos closes debut $6.2B GP financing using novel 'self-liquidating' Keystone structure, expanding toolkit for sponsor-backed capital raises.
Moelis hires Evercore team to build LP-led secondaries practice as market heats up; LGT, Dawson, Pomona aggressively sourcing deals amid favorable pricing.
Bridgepoint acquires majority stake in Danish defense-tech UMag with founders staying on; Nordic Capital and Ardian also active in European infra/defense plays as regional spending accelerates.

Markets & Macro.lede: Bank earnings kick off a pivotal week as mega-cap PE and IB firms capitalize on a bifurcated market—mega-funds raising $519B while AI debt issuance spikes 99%.

bullets.text: Bank earnings season opens Tuesday with Street braced for high expectations; results could set tone for Q2 guidance amid mixed macro signals.
Top 20 PE firms pulled $519B in H1 2026, up 27% YoY, while smaller funds struggle—signals consolidation pressure and a two-tier market emerging.
US IB fees on track for best year since 2022 as M&A revival and AI-fueled DCM surge offset recent dry spells.
AI-related debt issuance soared 99% YoY as hyperscalers fund infrastructure, creating single-name and sector concentration risks in fixed-income portfolios.

Newsletters.lede: Big bank earnings dominate today's FIG focus, while ETF flows turn negative and AI competition heats up—plus niche products proliferate.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow; results will signal credit quality, deposit health, and consumer spending across all major business lines.
ETF flows turned negative in a rare week—equity, bond, and alternative products all saw redemptions; worth understanding what triggered the shift.
Alibaba's AI model ranks first globally; cheaper Chinese alternatives are eroding US tech dominance in AI, signaling competitive pressure on domestic players.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Five stories on competition, moats, and economic blind spots—from AI commoditization reshaping tech moats to suburbs' underrated value proposition.

bullets.text: Open-source AI now matches frontier models at 1/10th the cost, obliterating raw capability as a moat and forcing founders to compete on distribution, data, or integration instead.
Tyler Cowen's wild take: markets stay competitive only if P ≠ NP; if P = NP, firms can efficiently detect and sustain cartels, making collusion mathematically rational.
American suburbs deliver real value (affordability, schools, safety) that urban elites systematically undervalue—a cultural bias that obscures genuine economic logic.
Economic literacy, not self-interest, predicts support for pro-growth policies like free trade and immigration across the EU—knowledge matters more than personal gain.

all.lede: Big financial services M&A week: Mastercard mulls Vocalink exit, CalPERS snaps up Russell Investments, and mega-PE firms dominate fundraising as bank earnings season kicks off.

bullets.text: Mastercard exploring sale of Vocalink, the UK payments platform acquired from British banks in 2016—potential return to original owners signals strategic reset.
CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments; $580B pension system gains multi-asset manager while Russell ditches debt and converts to non-profit governance (Vanguard 2.0 playbook).
Top 20 PE firms raised $519B H1 2026 (+27% YoY) while sub-scale funds struggle; concentration risk signals consolidation pressure among smaller players.
Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings this week; credit quality, deposits, and consumer health in focus as market braced for disappointment.
